item.title: Socioeconomic disparities in biologic persistence among patients with inflammatory bowel disease are associated with increased health care utilization.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Biologic persistence, defined as the total uninterrupted time on a biologic agent, is associated with improved outcomes in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, social determinants of health (...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease and risk of perineal injury in primiparous vaginal births: a retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The association between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) without active perineal disease and the risk of perineal trauma during vaginal birth remains unclear. Thus, we aimed to determine whether IB...

item.title: Entering a new GALAXI: The importance of long-term data in Crohn's disease clinical trials.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Crohn’s disease is a chronic, lifelong condition, but there has been a notable absence of long-term data from clinical trials, with outcomes typically focused on shorter-term outcomes at 6 months, ...

item.title: Prevalence, incidence and mortality of pediatric inflammatory bowel disease in Catalonia (EPIBDCat).
item.description: Revista espanola de enfermedades digestivas - The epidemiologic status of pediatric inflammatory bowel disease (PIBD) has not been widely studied in Western Europe. The prevalence and incidence trends of the disease are largely unknown. The ai...

item.title: Utility of T2* and DWI-IVIM to distinguish inflammatory and noninflammatory strictures in Crohn's disease: a prospective cross-sectional cohort study.

item.title: Therapy Persistence and Dose Escalation of Advanced Treatments in First and Second Line of Ulcerative Colitis and Crohn's Disease - A Retrospective Cohort Analysis of Healthcare Claims Data.

item.title: Incidence and Neoplastic Risk Associated with Colonic Stricture in Paediatric-Onset Crohn's Disease: A Population-based Study.

item.title: Therapeutic Promise of the Panax Genus in Ulcerative Colitis: Unraveling the Multitarget Mechanisms of Saponins and Polysaccharides.
item.description: Journal of agricultural and food chemistry - Review - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease with a rising global incidence. Panax genus plants, recognized for their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, present a...

item.title: Risk of autoimmune and rheumatic diseases in breast cancer patients with silicone breast implants.

item.title: Incidence and co-occurrence of six autoimmune diseases in childhood: a population-based cohort study in Norway.
item.description: The Lancet. Child & adolescent health - Autoimmune diseases are among the most common chronic diseases in childhood. We aimed to determine the incidence and co-occurrence of type 1 diabetes, inflammatory bowel disease, juvenile idiopathi...

item.title: Difference in the risk of gastrointestinal manifestations between peritoneal and hemodialysis patients: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: PeerJ - Meta-analysis - A large number of patients on dialysis have gastrointestinal (GI) manifestations. However, it remains unclear whether dialysis modality affects their prevalence. We present the first systematic rev...

item.title: The Fabrication, Characterization, and Ulcerative Colitis Treatment of Colonic Targeting Microspheres Loading Baicalein Nanocrystals Based on the "Nano in Micro" Strategy.
